data.Introduction to Python Programming: This course covers the fundamentals of Python programming language including variables, data types, control flow, functions, and object-oriented programming. Students will build real-world projects by the end of the course. Program Advisor: Dr. Sarah Mitchell, Department of Computer Science, available at sarah.mitchell@university.edu.
data.Data Structures and Algorithms: An in-depth exploration of core data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs. Covers algorithm design, complexity analysis, sorting, and searching techniques. Program Advisor: Prof. James Carter, Department of Software Engineering, available at james.carter@university.edu.
data.Machine Learning Fundamentals: Introduction to supervised and unsupervised learning, regression, classification, clustering, and neural networks. Hands-on experience with scikit-learn and TensorFlow. Program Advisor: Dr. Priya Nair, Department of Artificial Intelligence, available at priya.nair@university.edu.
data.Web Development with React: Comprehensive guide to building modern web applications using React, including hooks, state management with Redux, REST API integration, and deployment best practices. Program Advisor: Prof. David Chen, Department of Web Technologies, available at david.chen@university.edu.
data.Cloud Computing with AWS: Covers core AWS services including EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, and VPC. Students learn to design scalable, fault-tolerant cloud architectures following AWS Well-Architected Framework. Program Advisor: Dr. Michael Torres, Department of Cloud Infrastructure, available at michael.torres@university.edu.
data.Database Design and SQL: Explores relational database design, normalization, SQL querying, indexing, transactions, and performance tuning. Includes hands-on labs with PostgreSQL and MySQL. Program Advisor: Prof. Linda Zhao, Department of Data Engineering, available at linda.zhao@university.edu.
data.DevOps and CI/CD Pipelines: Covers DevOps principles, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and infrastructure as code using Terraform. Focus on automating build, test, and deployment pipelines. Program Advisor: Dr. Robert Kim, Department of Systems Engineering, available at robert.kim@university.edu.
data.Cybersecurity Essentials: Introduction to network security, cryptography, threat modeling, vulnerability assessment, and ethical hacking. Prepares students for foundational security certifications. Program Advisor: Prof. Angela Brooks, Department of Cybersecurity, available at angela.brooks@university.edu.
data.Microservices Architecture with Spring B: Deep dive into designing and building microservices using Java Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, service discovery, API gateways, distributed tracing, and event-driven communication via Kafka. Program Advisor: Dr. Nathan Patel, Department of Enterprise Architecture, available at nathan.patel@university.edu.
data.Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learnin: Advanced course covering deep learning architectures including CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, and LLMs. Topics include model training, fine-tuning, and deploying AI systems at scale. Program Advisor: Prof. Emily Tan, Department of Artificial Intelligence Research, available at emily.tan@university.edu.
